headline: Is Northrop Grumman Expanding Its Presence in the SEWIP Market?
summary: NOC expands its SEWIP role with a $312.3M Navy contract, advancing electronic warfare systems for future naval threat detection and defense.

headline: 3 Resilient Defense Stocks to Buy Now
summary: Defense primes are doing what they were built to do: compound through political noise. With the FY2027 Department of War budget request landing at $1.45 trillion, a $440.9 billion or 44% increase from the FY 2026 enacted level, and NATO members committing to 5% of GDP on defense by 2035, the multi-year demand picture has ... 3 Resilient Defense Stocks to Buy Now

headline: Defense Stocks Show Split Personality: Growth vs. Value Divergence Widens
summary: Defense stocks show a split: growth names like Howmet Aerospace and Curtiss-Wright command high valuations, while value plays Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman trade cheap with strong profitability but weak momentum.

headline: CSG Enters the Next Phase of Global Growth: Strengthening Its Leadership Team with Top Defence Executives
summary: Just a few years ago, CSG was predominantly Czech and Slovak. Today, it is drawing senior talent from leading defence companies including Rheinmetall, Northrop Grumman, Raytheon, BAE Systems, General Dynamics and Kongsberg. Foreign acquisitions, rapid growth and its stock market listing have turned CSG into one of the most attractive destinations for experienced managers in the global defence industry. “I think people are attracted above all by the opportunity to truly build something.
